stories.title: **Headline:** De La Cruz 2-HR Night Sets Reds Switch-Hitter Record — Cincinnati Beats Rays 12-6

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Elly De La Cruz: 2 HR, 5 RBI, 1 SB last night. His 6th career multi-HR game passed Pete Rose for the most by a switch-hitter in Reds history. Cincinnati scored the first 9 runs, won 12-6 over the Rays. The Reds are in first place. #Reds

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Elly De La Cruz just broke a Pete Rose record and barely anyone is talking about it. Six career multi-homer games — more than any switch-hitter in Reds history. And he added a stolen base. The Reds are COOKING. #Reds #MLB

article.headline: Elly De La Cruz Hits Two Home Runs, Passes Pete Rose in Reds History — Cincinnati Beats Tampa Bay 12-6

stories.title: **Headline:** Ohtani Pitches Best Start of the Year, Dodgers Lose Anyway — 2-1 to Astros

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Shohei Ohtani: 7 IP, 8 K, 0 walks, 89 pitches. His longest start as a Dodger. The offense managed 1 run. Final: Astros 2, Dodgers 1. Even a historically good start couldn't save LA's bats. His ERA is still 0.97. #Dodgers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Ohtani went 122 batters without allowing a home run this season. Then Christian Walker and Braden Shewmake took him deep in the same game. Still gave up 2 runs in 7 innings. Still lost 2-1. The Dodgers offense needs to show up. #Dodgers

article.headline: Ohtani Gives Everything — 7 IP, 8 K, 0 BB — and the Dodgers Lose 2-1 to Houston Anyway

stories.title: **Headline:** Nationals End Atlanta's 6-Game Win Streak — James Wood, García Jr. Power 11-4 Rout

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: The Washington Nationals ended the Braves' 6-game win streak Tuesday — 11-4. James Wood homered. Luis García Jr. had 4 hits. Atlanta is still 25-11 with an 8-game NL East lead. But nobody is unbeatable. #Braves #Nationals #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Washington beat Atlanta 11-4 to snap a 6-game Braves win streak. James Wood is playing like a star. The Nats may be building something. #Nationals #MLB

article.headline: Washington Ends Atlanta's 6-Game Winning Streak — James Wood Homers in 11-4 Nationals Win

stories.title: **Headline:** Sal Stewart Named NL Rookie of the Month — Reds' Infielder Leads ROY Race

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Sal Stewart was just named NL Rookie of the Month. Through April: .281, 9 HR, 29 RBI in just 31 games. He also leads the NL Rookie of the Year race. The Reds didn't just get good — they got a generational rookie. #Reds #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** Judge and Murakami Tied at 14 HRs — The AL Home Run Race Heats Up

x_posts.label: Post 5-1
x_posts.text: Aaron Judge: 14 HR. Munetaka Murakami: 14 HR. Tied for the MLB lead. Both on pace for 60+ homers over a full season. The home run race of 2026 is officially on. Who wins it? #Yankees #WhiteSox #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 5-2
x_posts.text: Murakami's first 12 extra-base hits this season have all been home runs. That's a record since 1900. Judge has 11 HRs in his last 21 games. They're both at 14. This is going to be a fun race to watch. #MLB

article.headline: Tied at 14: Aaron Judge and Munetaka Murakami Are Racing Toward History

stories.title: **Headline:** Tigers Rotation in Crisis — Skubal Out, Mize Out, Verlander Out

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text: The Detroit Tigers are now without Tarik Skubal (2-3 months), Casey Mize (IL), Justin Verlander (IL), and Reese Olson (season). Their rotation is Framber Valdez, Jack Flaherty, and hope. #Tigers #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** The Early Season MLB Landscape — Four Divisions, Four Stories

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text: Six weeks in, here's the MLB landscape: Braves lead baseball (25-11). Yankees leading the AL East (24-11). Reds surging in the NL Central. Ohtani ERA: 0.97. Judge and Murakami tied at 14 HR. It's been a great early season. #MLB
